questions.question: Which social platform consistently produces the highest Revenue Per Follower (RPF) for creator businesses?

options.text: Instagram -- because visual content drives more purchasing behavior
TikTok -- because the algorithm provides the largest organic reach
Email list -- producing 5-20x more revenue per contact than any social platform
YouTube -- because long-form content builds the deepest trust

questions.explanation: Email consistently produces 5-20x more revenue per contact than any social platform. The benchmarks show email at $2.00-10.00 annual RPF (with top performers at $20+), compared to LinkedIn at $0.50-2.00, YouTube at $0.20-1.00, and TikTok at just $0.01-0.10. This is why every serious creator builds an email list regardless of which platforms they use.

questions.question: The Audience Quality Score (AQS) framework measures 5 dimensions. A creator scores 2.5 on their AQS. What does this indicate?

options.text: Their audience is not commercially viable and they need to attract an entirely different audience
They have emerging commercial potential -- the audience needs more education and nurturing before buying premium offers
They have a strong commercial audience and the problem is their offers or sales process
They have an elite audience and should optimize their ascension ladder immediately

questions.explanation: An AQS of 2.0-3.0 indicates emerging commercial potential. The audience has some of the right characteristics but needs more education and nurturing before they will buy premium offers. This is different from AQS 3.0-4.0 (strong commercial audience where offers/sales process are the issue) or below 2.0 (not commercially viable).

questions.question: A ConvertKit study found that creators with fewer than 1,000 subscribers who focused on engagement earned $4.82 per subscriber annually, while creators with 10,000-50,000 subscribers who focused on growth earned $0.73 per subscriber. What principle does this illustrate?

options.text: Smaller lists are always more profitable than larger lists
Email marketing does not scale beyond 10,000 subscribers
Per-subscriber value drops when you prioritize growth over engagement, because new subscribers dilute list quality
Creators should never grow beyond 1,000 subscribers to maximize revenue

questions.explanation: The data illustrates that prioritizing growth over engagement dilutes per-subscriber value. Every new subscriber lowers your Revenue Per Subscriber unless they are as engaged as your existing audience. This does not mean you should never grow -- it means growth must be balanced with engagement and audience quality to maintain healthy economics.

questions.question: Score your audience using the 5-dimension Audience Quality Score framework (Engagement Depth, Purchase History, Problem Awareness, Budget Capacity, Referral Behavior -- each 1-5). What is your total AQS? Which dimension scored lowest, and what specific strategy from the lesson would you use to improve it?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the student provided specific scores for each of the 5 AQS dimensions with honest self-assessment. Check that their improvement strategy matches their weakest dimension -- for example, if Budget Capacity is low, they should consider shifting their content to attract a more professional audience, not just posting more content. Encourage concrete next steps.
